remediation: GPO: Computer Config > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Account Policies > Password Policy > Enforce password history = 24 or more
GPO: Computer Config > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Account Policies > Password Policy > Maximum password age = 365 days or less
GPO: Set Maximum password age to 0 (never expire) or >= 365 days. NCSC recommends no forced expiry; change only on compromise.
GPO: Computer Config > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Account Policies > Password Policy > Minimum password age = 1 or more days
GPO: Computer Config > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Account Policies > Password Policy > Minimum password length = 14 or more characters
GPO: Computer Config > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Account Policies > Password Policy > Password must meet complexity requirements = Enabled
GPO: Computer Config > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Account Policies > Password Policy > Store passwords using reversible encryption = Disabled
GPO: Computer Config > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Account Policies > Account Lockout Policy > Account lockout duration = 15 or more minutes
GPO: Computer Config > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Account Policies > Account Lockout Policy > Account lockout threshold = 5 or fewer invalid attempts
GPO: Computer Config > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Account Policies > Account Lockout Policy > Reset account lockout counter after = 15 or more minutes
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > Remote Desktop Services > Remote Desktop Session Host > Connections > Allow users to connect remotely = Disabled
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > Remote Desktop Services > Remote Desktop Session Host > Security > Require use of specific security layer = SSL
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > Remote Desktop Services > Remote Desktop Session Host > Security > Set client connection encryption level = High Level
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > Remote Desktop Services > Remote Desktop Session Host > Security > Require NLA = Enabled
Run: net user Administrator /active:no  OR  GPO: Computer Config > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options > Accounts: Administrator account status = Disabled
Run: net user Guest /active:no  OR  GPO: Computer Config > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options > Accounts: Guest account status = Disabled
GPO: Computer Config > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Windows Firewall with Advanced Security > Domain Profile > Firewall state = On (recommended)
GPO: Computer Config > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Windows Firewall with Advanced Security > Private Profile > Firewall state = On (recommended)
GPO: Computer Config > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Windows Firewall with Advanced Security > Public Profile > Firewall state = On (recommended)
Run Windows Update immediately. Open Settings > Update & Security > Windows Update > Check for updates. Apply all pending patches.
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > Windows Update > Configure Automatic Updates = Enabled (4 - Auto download and schedule the install)
Run PowerShell: Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName SMB1Protocol -NoRestart  OR  Set registry: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanmanServer\Parameters\SMB1 = 0
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > AutoPlay Policies > Turn off Autoplay = Enabled (All drives)
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > AutoPlay Policies > Set the default behavior for AutoRun = Do not execute any autorun commands

GPO: Computer Config > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options > User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators = Prompt for consent on the secure desktop
GPO: Computer Config > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options > User Account Control: Run all administrators in Admin Approval Mode = Enabled
Registry: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\LmCompatibilityLevel = 5 (Send NTLMv2 response only. Refuse LM & NTLM)
Ensure Windows Defender is enabled and no third-party AV is interfering. Run: Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $false
Update Defender signatures: Run Update-MpSignature or check Windows Update. Signatures should be less than 7 days old.

Enable BitLocker: Run manage-bde -on C: -RecoveryPassword  OR  GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > BitLocker Drive Encryption > Operating System Drives > Require additional authentication at startup
Secure Boot must be enabled in UEFI/BIOS firmware settings. Reboot into firmware setup and enable Secure Boot.
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > Windows PowerShell > Turn on Script Block Logging = Enabled
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > Windows PowerShell > Turn on Module Logging = Enabled. Specify modules: *
PowerShell: Set-ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Force  OR  GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Windows Components > Windows PowerShell > Turn on Script Execution = Allow only signed scripts
Deploy AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC). GPO: Computer Config > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Application Control Policies > AppLocker
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > System > Credentials Delegation > Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials = Enabled
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Control Panel > Personalization > Enable screen saver = Enabled, with timeout <= 900 seconds and password protection enabled
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > Network > Network Connections > Prohibit use of Internet Connection Sharing = Enabled
GPO: Computer Config > Admin Templates > System > Exploit Protection > Use a common set of exploit protection settings. Deploy via: Set-ProcessMitigation -System -Enable DEP,SEHOP
Update the vulnerable application to the latest version immediately. Check vendor website or use application's built-in update mechanism.
Update stale applications (>30 days since last update). Check for updates via vendor website, application settings, or package manager.
